sanskrit:
पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे।
साङ्ख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्वकर्मणाम्॥
transliteration: pañcaitāni mahā-bāho kāraṇāni nibodha me | sāṅkhye kṛtānte proktāni siddhaye sarva-karmaṇām ||
literal_meaning: Five causes, O mighty-armed, hear from Me; declared in Sāṅkhya for the perfection of every action.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the five factors that together produce any action, inviting Arjuna to shift from ego-centric ownership to systemic awareness.
psychological_state: Cognitive defusion—observing the components of action without fusing with any single one.
trigger_scenario: A project manager blames herself for a failed launch; learning the ‘five-factor’ model helps her see the outcome as an interplay of context, team, tools, effort, and market forces—reducing shame and restoring agency.
anchor_text: See every deed as fivefold, not 'my' act alone.
